Block.Para: First Session, Forty-first Parliament,

STATUTES OF CANADA 2013
CHAPTER 7
An Act to enhance the financial accountability and transparency of First Nations
ASSENTED TO
27th MARCH, 2013
BILL C-27

Also available on the Parliament of Canada Web Site at the following address:
http://www.parl.gc.ca
SUMMARY
This enactment enhances the financial accountability and transparency of First Nations.

60-61-62 ELIZABETH II
芒聙聰芒聙聰芒聙聰芒聙聰芒聙聰芒聙聰
CHAPTER 7
An Act to enhance the financial accountability and transparency of First Nations
[Assented to 27th March, 2013]
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:
SHORT TITLE
1. This Act may be cited as the First Nations Financial Transparency Act.
INTERPRETATION
2. The following definitions apply in this Act.

Block.MarginalNote:
芒聙聹consolidated financial statements芒聙聺
脗芦 脙漏tats financiers consolid脙漏s 脗禄
Block.Para: 芒聙聹consolidated financial statements芒聙聺 means the financial statements of a First Nation 芒聙聰 prepared in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles 芒聙聰 in which the assets, liabilities, equity, income, expenses and cash flows of the First Nation and of those entities that are required by those principles to be included are presented as those of a single economic entity, as if the First Nation were a government reporting on its financial information.

Block.MarginalNote:
芒聙聹council芒聙聺
脗芦 conseil 脗禄
Block.Para: 芒聙聹council芒聙聺 has the same meaning as 芒聙聹council of the band芒聙聺 in subsection 2(1) of the Indian Act.

Block.MarginalNote:
芒聙聹entity芒聙聺
脗芦 entit脙漏 脗禄
Block.Para: 芒聙聹entity芒聙聺 means a corporation or a partnership, a joint venture or any other unincorporated association or organization.

Block.MarginalNote:
芒聙聹expenses芒聙聺
脗芦 d脙漏penses 脗禄
Block.Para: 芒聙聹expenses芒聙聺 includes the costs of transportation, accommodation, meals, hospitality and incidental expenses.

Block.MarginalNote:
芒聙聹First Nation芒聙聺
脗芦 premi脙篓re nation 脗禄
Block.Para: 芒聙聹First Nation芒聙聺 means a band, as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Indian Act, but does not include a band that is party to a comprehensive self-government agreement given effect by an Act of Parliament.

Block.MarginalNote:
芒聙聹member芒聙聺
脗芦 membre 脗禄
Block.Para: 芒聙聹member芒聙聺 has the same meaning as 芒聙聹member of a band芒聙聺 in subsection 2(1) of the Indian Act.

Block.MarginalNote:
芒聙聹Minister芒聙聺
脗芦 ministre 脗禄
Block.Para: 芒聙聹Minister芒聙聺 means the Minister of Indian Affairs and Northern Development.

Block.MarginalNote:
芒聙聹remuneration芒聙聺
脗芦 r脙漏mun脙漏ration 脗禄
Block.Para: 芒聙聹remuneration芒聙聺 means any salaries, wages, commissions, bonuses, fees, honoraria and dividends and any other monetary benefits 芒聙聰 other than the reimbursement of expenses 芒聙聰 and non-monetary benefits.
GENERAL

3. The purpose of this Act is to enhance the financial accountability and transparency of First Nations by requiring the preparation and public disclosure of their audited consolidated financial statements and of the schedules of remuneration paid and expenses reimbursed to a First Nation芒聙聶s chief and each of its councillors 芒聙聰 acting in their capacity as such and in any other capacity, including their personal capacity 芒聙聰 by the First Nation and by any entity that, in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles, is required to be consolidated with the First Nation.
4. This Act applies in respect of every First Nation芒聙聶s financial year that begins after the day on which this Act comes into force.
FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AND SCHEDULE OF REMUNERATION AND EXPENSES
Preparation and Independent Audit

5. (1) A First Nation must maintain its accounts and prepare its consolidated financial statements annually in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles, the primary sources of which are the handbooks 芒聙聰 including the handbook respecting public sector accounting 芒聙聰 of the Canadian Institute of Chartered Accountants, or its successor, as they are amended from time to time.

(2) A First Nation芒聙聶s consolidated financial statements must be audited, in accordance with the generally accepted auditing standards of the Canadian Institute of Chartered Accountants, or its successor, by an independent auditor who is a member in good standing of a corporation, institute or association of accountants incorporated under an Act of the legislature of a province.

6. (1) A First Nation must annually prepare a document entitled 芒聙聹Schedule of Remuneration and Expenses芒聙聺 that sets out, separately, the remuneration paid and the expenses reimbursed to its chief and each of its councillors 芒聙聰 acting in their capacity as such and in any other capacity, including their personal capacity 芒聙聰 by the First Nation and by any entity that, in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles, is required to be consolidated with the First Nation.
(2) The schedule does not form part of the consolidated financial statements.
(3) An auditor芒聙聶s report or a review engagement report, as the case may be, prepared by the auditor referred to in subsection 5(2), must accompany the schedule.
Disclosure
7. (1) A First Nation must, on the request of any of its members, provide the member with copies of any of the following documents:
(a) its audited consolidated financial statements;
(b) the Schedule of Remuneration and Expenses;
(c) the auditor芒聙聶s written report respecting the consolidated financial statements; and
(d) the auditor芒聙聶s report or the review engagement report, as the case may be, respecting the Schedule of Remuneration and Expenses.
(2) The First Nation must provide the copies without delay, but has until 120 days after the end of the financial year in question to provide them if the request is received within those 120 days.
(3) A First Nation may charge a fee for providing the copies, but the fee must not exceed the cost of the service.

Block.MarginalNote: Internet site 芒聙聰 First Nation
Block.Para: 8. (1) A First Nation must publish the documents referred to in paragraphs 7(1)(a) to (d) on its Internet site, or cause those documents to be published on an Internet site, within 120 days after the end of each financial year.
(2) The documents referred to in subsection (1) must remain accessible to the public, on an Internet site, for at least 10 years.
(3) Publishing any document on an Internet site is insufficient to discharge the First Nation芒聙聶s duty to make copies of it available to its members who request that document.

9. The Minister must publish the documents referred to in paragraphs 7(1)(a) to (d) on the Department of Indian Affairs and Northern Development芒聙聶s Internet site without delay after the First Nation has provided him or her with those documents or they have been published under subsection 8(1).
COURT REMEDIES AND ADMINISTRATIVE MEASURES
Orders

Block.MarginalNote: Application by member of First Nation
Block.Para: 10. If a First Nation fails to provide copies of any document under section 7, any member of that First Nation may apply to a superior court for an order requiring the council to carry out the duties under that section within the period specified by the court.

11. If a First Nation fails to publish any document under section 8, any person, including the Minister, may apply to a superior court for an order requiring the council to carry out the duties under that section within the period specified by the court.

12. An application for an order in respect of documents referred to in paragraphs 7(1)(a) to (d) for the most recent financial year may only be made after the expiry of 120 days after the end of that financial year.
Administrative Measures
13. (1) If a First Nation is in breach of any duty imposed on it under sections 5 to 8, the Minister may take one or more of the following measures:
(a) require the council to develop an appropriate action plan to remedy the breach;

(b) withhold moneys payable as a grant or contribution to the First Nation under an agreement that is in force on the day on which the breach occurs and that is entered into by the First Nation and Her Majesty in right of Canada as represented by the Minister, solely or in combination with other ministers of the Crown, until the First Nation has complied with its duty; or
(c) terminate any agreement referred to in paragraph (b).
(2) An amount withheld under paragraph (1)(b) is deemed to be an amount that is due or owing for the purposes of section 37.1 of the Financial Administration Act.
Published under authority of the Speaker of the House of Commons
脗聞
